title: The Century Report: March 22 2026
description:
TCR 03/22/26: Code Writes Itself Now

OpenAI cofounder Andrej Karpathy disclosed yesterday that he hasn't written a single line of code since December, describing an inversion from 80% human-written to 80% agent-written software within weeks as a "state of psychosis" - while OpenAI simultaneously announced plans to nearly double its workforce to 8,000, hiring thousands of humans whose job is helping enterprises absorb what these systems can do. AI compute tokens are entering Silicon Valley compensation alongside salary and equity, with some engineers consuming more in compute than they earn in cash - reframing employment around the direction of intelligence rather than the execution of tasks. The Iran conflict severed a third of the world's helium supply when strikes damaged Qatar's Ras Laffan facility, threatening semiconductor fabrication plants in Asia within weeks as spot prices doubled and 200 specialized cryogenic containers sit stranded in the Middle East. Microsoft signed a letter of intent for 1.35 GW of off-grid, gas-powered data center capacity in West Virginia - one of 46 such facilities being built nationwide to bypass grid queues that can't keep pace with compute demand. University of Konstanz physicists published findings in Nature Materials demonstrating friction between surfaces that never touch, breaking Amontons' 300-year-old law through collective magnetic behavior.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-22-2026/
